articles.title: 2024 Guide to Federal and State Solar Incentives
articles.content: This comprehensive 2024 guide breaks down the federal solar Investment Tax Credit (ITC), state-level rebates, and local utility incentives. Learn how to stack incentives, understand eligibility, and estimate your out-of-pocket costs before and after rebates...
articles.summary: Understand how the 2024 federal ITC, state rebates, and local incentives work together to cut your solar costs.

articles.title: Solar Incentives for San Francisco Homeowners (ZIP 94110)
articles.content: If you live in ZIP code 94110, you may qualify for multiple incentive layers: the federal ITC, California state programs, and PG&E-specific bill credits. This article walks through typical savings for a $150/month bill and how incentives affect system sizing...
articles.summary: See which incentives apply to homes in ZIP 94110 and how they impact system sizing and payback.

articles.title: Rebates and Tax Credits for Small Business Solar
articles.content: Retailers, offices, and light industrial facilities can often combine the federal ITC with bonus depreciation and local business rebates. We explain how a 50–100 kW system can qualify and what documentation you’ll need for your CPA...
articles.summary: How commercial solar incentives reduce payback periods for 50–100 kW systems.

articles.title: How Incentives Differ When You Lease vs. Buy Solar
articles.content: When you buy solar, you generally claim the tax credit and depreciation benefits yourself. With leases and PPAs, the financing company often claims the ITC and passes savings to you as a lower rate. This article explains how our 20-year comparison calculator models these differences...
articles.summary: Understand who gets the tax credit when you lease or finance a solar system and how it affects long-term savings.

articles.title: Are Solar Garden Lights Worth It for Your Yard?
articles.content: Solar garden lights are a low-commitment way to start saving energy outdoors. We compare brightness, battery life, and weather resistance, and explain what to look for in ratings and reviews before you buy...
articles.summary: What to check before buying solar garden lights and how much energy they can save.

articles.title: How to Choose the Right Rooftop Solar Package
articles.content: System size, price, and warranty length all matter when comparing rooftop solar packages. This guide shows how a longer warranty can offset a slightly higher upfront cost and why kW capacity becomes your tie-breaker...
articles.summary: Use warranty years and kW system size to compare residential solar packages with confidence.

articles.title: Home Solar Maintenance Basics: What to Expect Each Year
articles.content: Most modern solar systems are low-maintenance, but an annual plan can catch small issues early. Learn what inspections include, how much they cost per month, and which tasks you can safely DIY...
articles.summary: Understand typical annual maintenance tasks and how maintenance plans are priced.

articles.title: How to Prepare for a 30-Minute Virtual Solar Consultation
articles.content: A focused 30-minute virtual consultation can answer most of your solar questions. We outline what photos to have ready, which bill details matter, and how roof type (like a 1-story home) affects your options...
articles.summary: Make the most of your 30-minute virtual solar consultation with this simple checklist.

articles.title: Solar + Battery Bundles: How Much Storage Do You Need?
articles.content: Pairing ~6 kW of solar with 10–20 kWh of battery storage can keep essential loads running through outages. We explain how our bundle builder estimates runtime and how financing options affect your monthly payment...
articles.summary: Estimate the right solar + battery size for your home and budget.

articles.title: How to Read Solar Product Ratings and Reviews
articles.content: Star ratings can hide important differences in durability and performance. Learn how to spot high-value solar garden lights by combining price, rating (4.0+), and verified reviews...
articles.summary: Use ratings, reviews, and pricing together to find the best-value solar products.

articles.title: A Brief History of the Solar Investment Tax Credit (ITC)
articles.content: The federal ITC has been extended and adjusted multiple times. This article provides a timeline of percentage changes, major policy updates, and what the latest legislation means for homeowners going solar in the next few years...
articles.summary: See how the federal ITC has changed over time and what rate applies if you install this year.

articles.title: Estimated Solar Savings in Denver (ZIP 80210)
articles.content: Homes in ZIP code 80210 see above-average sun hours and competitive utility rates. We walk through an example $180/month bill and compare 20-year savings for buying vs. leasing solar using our calculator...
articles.summary: Example 20-year savings for buying vs. leasing solar in Denver’s 80210 ZIP code.

articles.title: What to Expect from a Commercial Solar Site Assessment
articles.content: From roof measurements to electrical panel checks, a commercial site assessment ensures your 50–100 kW system is feasible and code-compliant. Retail businesses learn what data to prepare and how long visits typically take...
articles.summary: Step-by-step overview of the commercial solar site assessment process for retail businesses.

articles.title: Checklist: Maximizing Your Solar Rebates and Credits
articles.content: Use this pre-install checklist to avoid missing out on rebates: confirm program deadlines, reserve funds where required, and gather utility account details. We include printable and digital versions you can save to your reading list...
articles.summary: A practical checklist to ensure you capture every available solar rebate and credit.

articles.title: How Time-of-Use Rates Change Solar Savings
articles.content: Utilities increasingly use time-of-use (TOU) rates, which can increase the value of solar and batteries. Learn how our savings calculator models TOU schedules and why batteries can shift your solar production into peak-price hours...
articles.summary: Understand how TOU rate structures affect your modeled solar and battery savings.

articles.title: Comparing Solar Maintenance Plans: Annual vs. Quarterly
articles.content: Should you choose an annual, semi-annual, or quarterly maintenance plan? We compare typical monthly pricing under $40, what each visit includes, and which plan makes sense for different system ages...
articles.summary: Decide whether annual, semi-annual, or quarterly maintenance gives you the best value.

maintenance_plans.name: SolarCare Annual Basic
maintenance_plans.description: One comprehensive annual inspection, performance report, and panel cleaning for residential systems up to 10 kW.

maintenance_plans.name: SolarCare Annual Plus
maintenance_plans.description: Annual visit plus priority scheduling, monitoring alerts review, and extended inverter checks.

maintenance_plans.name: SolarCare Semi-Annual Standard
maintenance_plans.description: Two visits per year with detailed production analysis and preventive maintenance for systems up to 15 kW.

maintenance_plans.name: SolarCare Quarterly Premium
maintenance_plans.description: Four visits per year for large or high-visibility systems needing frequent cleanings and inspections.

maintenance_plans.name: One-Time System Checkup
maintenance_plans.description: A single on-site inspection and performance check, ideal before selling a home or after a major storm.
